channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Morocco’s social security database hacked and leaked on Telegram
item.description: Morocco‘s social security agency said troves of data were stolen from its systems in a cyberattack this week that resulted in personal information being leaked on the messaging app Telegram.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Trump’s revenge politics has a new target: the cybersecurity official who defied him in 2020
item.description: President Donald Trump on Wednesday ordered federal officials to investigate the former head of the country’s top cybersecurity agency and revoke his security clearances, years after he first disputed Trump’s election fraud claims.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Vector Institute aims to clear up confusion about AI model performance
item.description: AI models are advancing at a dizzying clip, with builders boasting ever more impressive performance with each iteration.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Arm backs big data center ambitions with migration tools
item.description: Arm Holdings expects its architecture will account for half of the data center CPU market share by the end of this year, and to help achieve those goals it has launched a batch of new migration tools for application porting.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump Administration's Cybersecurity Policy in Focus: Review of Key Regulations Looms
item.description: GOP lawmakers call for review of existing and future cyber regulations, citing concerns over duplication and redundancy across the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: A quick win for DOGE? Make procurement competitive again
item.description: If DOGE is looking for a high-value, high-return target, the software procurement process is an easy win. The post A quick win for DOGE? Make procurement competitive again first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Apple has few incentives to start making iPhones in U.S., despite Trump’s trade war with China
item.description: It would take several years and cost billions of dollars to build new plants in the U.S., and then confront Apple with economic forces that could triple the price of an iPhone, threatening to torpedo sales of its marquee product.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Hackers breach Morocco's social security database in an unprecedented cyberattack
item.description: RABAT, Morocco (AP) — Morocco 's social security agency said troves of data were stolen from its systems in a cyberattack this week that resulted in personal information being leaked on the messaging app Telegram.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Nintendo says its divisive Game-Key Cards will help 'future-proof' the Switch 2
item.description: Nintendo wants to ensure that the Switch 2 is "future-proof", and it's trying to achieve that with its new Game-Key Cards.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: I really wanted these viral pop star emojis to arrive with iOS 18.5, but it was never going to happen – here’s why
item.description: A viral post convinced millions that Apple was bringing pop star emojis to iOS 18.5. Turns out it was all a lie.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: The new PS Portal system update brings a slew of improvements, including the ability to capture gameplay in cloud sessions, a sort feature, and more
item.description: A new PlayStation Portal update arrives today, bringing a ton of new features aimed at improving the user experience while using Cloud Game Streaming Beta.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: One Tech Tip: Locking down your device when crossing borders
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Planning an international trip? Travelers should prepare for the possibility of extra scrutiny of their phones when crossing borders, especially when entering the United States. The Canadian government warned travelers in a recent travel advisory that U.S.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cisco: Latest news and insights
item.description: Cisco is the dominant vendor in enterprise networking, and under CEO Chuck Robbins, it continues to shake things up. Its most recent mega-acquisition is its $28 billion purchase of Splunk, a bold move that adds revenue opportunities in observability, data analytics, AIOps, and genAI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft says it's 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects, including $1B plan for Ohio
item.description: Microsoft said it is “slowing or pausing” some of its data center construction, including a $1 billion project in Ohio, the latest sign that the demand for artificial intelligence technology that drove a massive infrastructure expansion might not need quite as many powerful computers as expected.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tesla is launching sales in Saudi Arabia, where the obstacles do not (yet) include Musk protests
item.description: Tesla starts selling cars in Saudi Arabia on Thursday, a country where on a 900-kilometre (559 mile) stretch of its main east-west highway linking the capital Riyadh and the holy city of Mecca there isn’t a single charging station.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Former Facebook executive tells Senate committee company undermined US national security with China
item.description: Sarah Wynn-Williams, a former executive at Meta and author of an explosive insider account of her time at the social media giant, testified before the Senate Judiciary Committee Wednesday, accusing the company of undermining national security and briefing China on U.S.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Fortinet embeds AI capabilities across Security Fabric platform
item.description: Fortinet has expanded support for AI across its core cybersecurity platform to protect enterprise customers from evolving threats and simplify network and security operations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Agentic AI – Ongoing coverage of its impact on the enterprise
item.description: Over the next few years, agentic AI is expected to bring not only rapid technological breakthroughs, but a societal transformation, redefining how we live, work and interact with the world. And this shift is happening quickly.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: UALink releases inaugural GPU interconnect specification
item.description: The multivendor Ultra Accelerator Link (UALink) consortium has published its first specification aimed at delivering an open standard interconnect for AI clusters – and providing an alternative to Nvidia’s proprietary NVLink.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta helped build China’s DeepSeek: Whistleblower testimony
item.description: Former Meta executive Sarah Wynn-Williams is set to testify before the US Senate Judiciary Committee on Wednesday, revealing how the company’s AI model, Llama, played a critical role in accelerating China’s AI capabilities — particularly contributing to the rise of DeepSeek.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google adds natural language query capabilities to AlloyDB
item.description: Google is enhancing AlloyDB, its fully managed database-as-a-service (DBaaS), to help developers build applications underpinned by generative AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: DoubleClickjacking hack turns double-clicks into account takeovers
item.description: Double-clicking is something we all do, often without giving it a second thought. But it could be giving hackers permission to do something dangerous.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft employees protest at 50th anniversary party over Israel contract
item.description: A pro-Palestinian protest by Microsoft employees interrupted the company’s 50th anniversary celebration Friday, the latest backlash over the tech industry’s work to supply artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: An AI avatar tried to argue a case before a New York court. The judges weren't having it
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took only seconds for the judges on a New York appeals court to realize that the man addressing them from a video screen — a person about to present an argument in a lawsuit — not only had no law degree, but didn't exist at all.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How to remotely reset your router: Steps for easy troubleshooting
item.description: You can reset your internet router remotely if you're not home when your power goes out. Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how this can be done.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI vs. pro gambler's $1 million March Madness bet coming down to Duke and Houston
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — The $1 million March Madness wager between a pro gambler and an artificial-intelligence site will be decided by the winner of the Duke-Houston game in the Final Four. The Vegas bookies project the gambler, who picked 5 1/2-point favorite Duke, should win.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: High-tech canopy provides off-grid power and water
item.description: The EO Canopy is a self-sustaining, solar-powered camping platform designed to provide all the comforts of home while completely off the grid.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
